author: doll-agent
content:
Add MaskedArray::sort_axis(axis) returning MaskedArray<T, IxDyn> with the input shape preserved. Each lane along axis is sorted independently: unmasked ascending, masked at end. The existing sort() (no axis, flattens to 1-D) keeps its semantics for backward compat.

Note: numpy.ma.compressed always returns 1-D, so no axis parameter is needed there - the issue title was slightly misleading on that front.

3 tests added: per-row 2D sort with masked tails, per-column 2D sort with mixed mask, axis-out-of-bounds rejection.
